Name: Ciudad de las Artes y las Ciencias
Address: Av. del Professor López Piñero 7 Valencia
Tips: Santiago Calatrava's spectacular bone-white complex on dried riverbed. Exterior completely free and extraordinary at dusk. L'Oceanogràfic (Europe's largest aquarium) inside takes half a day.

Name: Valencia Cathedral + Holy Grail
Address: Plaza de la Reina s/n Valencia
Tips: THE Valencia sight for families who love churches. Houses the Holy Grail (1st-century Roman alabaster chalice). Climb El Miguelete tower for best old-town view. Open until 18:30.

Name: Mercado Central
Address: Pl. de la Ciutat de Bruges s/n Valencia
Tips: One of the most beautiful market buildings in Europe (1928 Modernista). Go before 11:00. Stalls of paella ingredients fresh seafood saffron. Spectacular central dome.

Name: Barrio del Carmen
Tips: Valencia's medieval quarter. Bohemian street art and independent bars. Best for an evening wander on arrival. Plaça del Tossal and Plaça del Negrito are local gathering spots at night.

Name: L'Oceanogràfic
Address: C/ de José Ortega Gasset 100 Valencia
Tips: Europe's largest aquarium inside the City of Arts and Sciences complex. 2.5-3 hrs. If family did Lisbon Oceanário this is bigger with different species.

Name: Jardins del Turia
Tips: 9km of gardens in the old Turia riverbed. World's longest urban park. Gulliver Park (free giant Gulliver sculpture with slides — kids love it). Cycling walking playgrounds.

Name: La Malvarrosa Beach
Tips: Valencia's city beach — birthplace of paella. Metro to Neptú stop 20 min from centre. Wide and sandy. Good for early morning swim before train on Aug 28.

Name: Llotja de la Seda (Silk Exchange)
Tips: UNESCO Gothic secular hall (1482-1548). One of finest Gothic buildings in Europe. Helical columns in trading hall are extraordinary. Almost empty even in August. Go early.

Name: Real Basilica de la Virgen
Address: Pl. de la Mare de Déu Valencia
Tips: Baroque basilica with frescoed dome next to Cathedral. Plaza de la Virgen with Roman fountain is one of best squares in Valencia. Very atmospheric.

Name: Horchatería Santa Catalina
Tips: The reference horchatería of Valencia since 1936. Horchata de chufa + fartons (soft dipping pastry). Essential Valencia experience. Open from 08:00.

Name: Estació del Nord
Tips: Beautiful 1917 Valencian Modernisme train station — orange blossom motifs everywhere. The departure point for the Euromed 11:25 AM train to Barcelona Aug 28.

Name: Mercado de Colón
Tips: Beautiful 1916 Modernist iron building turned into food and drink hall. Good option for drinks and light bites. More refined than a market.
